dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9274 IH: Gun Violence Prevention and Public Safety Database Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Foushee introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To direct the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention to establish a gun violence prevention and public safety database.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Gun Violence Prevention and Public Safety Database Act of 2026.

section.header: Gun Violence Prevention and Public Safety Database

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention shall establish a publicly accessible database on the internet website of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention of research relating to gun violence in the United States meeting the criteria described under subsection (b).

Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, the Director shall publish on the internet website of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention a clear description of criteria for research to be included in the database established under subsection (a). At minimum the research—

paragraph.text: with respect to baseline evidentiary requirements shall—

subparagraph.text: be data collected that presents an original analysis, systematic review, or data-driven findings;
describe the data sources or evidence relied upon; and
include a description of the methodology sufficient to understand how conclusions were reached;

paragraph.text: shall be published in a peer-reviewed journal, issued or commissioned by a Federal, State, or local government agency, or be produced by a research organization with an established review or editorial process; and
shall include any other information determined by the Director pursuant to subsection (c).

subsection.text: Any research included in the database established under subsection (a) shall include to the greatest extent possible—

paragraph.text: identification of the entity authoring such research; and
disclosure of any funding source or sponsorship of such research.

subsection.text: Research included on the database established under subsection (a) shall at minimum include research relating to gun violence from the—

paragraph.text: National Electronic Injury Surveillance System-All Injury Program;
National Hospital Ambulatory Medical Care Survey;
Behavioral Risk Factor and Surveillance System; and

subsection.text: The Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention shall update the database established under subsection (a) every six months pursuant to the criteria described under subsection (b).

Notwithstanding any other provision of law, Federal funds made available to the Department of Health and Human Services, including the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention and the National Institutes of Health, may be used to conduct or support research with respect to gun violence.
There is authorized to be appropriated $5,000,000 for each fiscal year 2026 through 2030 to carry out this Act.
